message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>We investigate the effects of social distancing in controlling the impact of the COVID-19 epidemic using a simple susceptible-infected-removed epidemic model. We show that an alternative or complementary approach based on targeted isolation of the vulnerable subpopulation may provide a more efficient and robust strategy at a lower economic and social cost within a shorter timeframe resulting in a collectively immune population.</jats:p>
title: Targeted adaptive isolation strategy for Covid-19 pandemic
